Severe storms in the NYC area have caused travel disruptions, downed trees, and power outages, while high winds have led to vehicle restrictions on the Staten Island bridge; additionally, antisemitic attacks are on the rise, prompting community concerns.

### Risks & Alerts

- Severe storms cause downed trees and power outages across New York and New Jersey. [↗](https://news.google.)
- High winds lead MTA to restrict vehicles on Staten Island bridge. [↗](https://news.google.)
- Driver killed while avoiding downed tree amid severe weather conditions in NYC. [↗](https://news.google.)
- Antisemitic graffiti found on Brooklyn building amid rising hate crimes in NYC. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMif0FVX3lxTE5vQm5CSWxfQWpnNk9BYlh6VjU2ZERlNFZmR1B2OXFTQnVZWnR2cGxtUjFWdlNOQzA3RGhPX3ozZnVETTUtRFFzUFNHY3ZLMWVyb25xUnFGYmZHRFZna0FlRW1aSDNMdDJmTVJ1RF9NNEwwSkdiREpBNFJtVTZqQzg)

### Civics & Politics

- NYC spends $81K per homeless person without significant results, raising concerns about resource allocation. - Proposed law may require New Yorkers to turn off outdoor lights by 11 PM nightly to reduce light pollution. - Amazon faces nearly $10M in unpaid fines for idling violations in New York City.
